basicDetails.schemeName: Training Program for A-HELP Women Workers - Uttarakhand Livestock Development Board

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme “Training Program for A-HELP Women Workers” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Livestock Development Board (ULDB) under the Department of Animal Husbandry, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide community-service-based skill development and employment opportunities for women in the field of animal husbandry.
schemeContent.benefits_md: Under this scheme, community-service-based job creation or job filling is carried out between the Animal Husbandry Department and Animal Husbandry by A-HELP workers.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide community-service-based skill development and employment opportunities for women in the field of animal husbandry. Eligible women members of Self-Help Groups under the Uttarakhand State Rural Livelihood Mission can apply.

children.text: The scheme “Training Program for A-HELP Women Workers” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Livestock Development Board (ULDB) under the Department of Animal Husbandry, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide community-service-based skill development and employment opportunities for women in the field of animal husbandry.
Under this scheme, community-service-based job creation or job filling is carried out between the Animal Husbandry Department and Animal Husbandry by A-HELP workers.
Eligible women selected by the Uttarakhand State Rural Livelihood Mission may apply.

applicationProcess.process_md: Eligible women selected by the Uttarakhand State Rural Livelihood Mission may apply.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The applicant must be a woman member of a self-help group formed under the Uttarakhand State Rural Livelihood Mission. 1. The applicant must have a minimum educational qualification of eighth pass. 1. The applicant must have completed 7 days of Pashu Sakhi training.

children.text: The applicant must be a woman member of a self-help group formed under the Uttarakhand State Rural Livelihood Mission.
The applicant must have a minimum educational qualification of eighth pass.
The applicant must have completed 7 days of Pashu Sakhi training.
